content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation of nnn  a1,a2,...ana_1,a_2,...a_na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,...an‚Äã. You will also be given another integer xxx between 111 and nnn.
In one move, you can swap the element xxx with an element at an odd number of places to the left or right of it. For example, if xxx is at position iii, then you can swap it with ai+1,ai+3,...a_{i+1},a_{i+3},...ai+1‚Äã,ai+3‚Äã,... and ai‚àí1,ai‚àí3,...a_{i-1},a_{i-3},...ai‚àí1‚Äã,ai‚àí3‚Äã,...

Your task is to sort the array in at most 10n10n10n moves or determine that it is impossible to sort the array
###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains two lines of input.
The first line contains two integers nnn and xxx.
The second line contains nnn space separated numbers a1,a2,...ana_1,a_2,...a_na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,...an‚Äã
###Output:
For each test case, output in the first line "YES" if it is possible to sort the array otherwise "NO."
If it is possible to sort the array, then in the next line output the number of moves mmm.
Then output mmm lines, consisting of two indexes iii and jjj to be swapped. (Note that either iii or jjj should be the index of xxx.
###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§10001 \leq T \leq 10001‚â§T‚â§1000
1‚â§n‚â§1051 \leq n \leq 10^51‚â§n‚â§105
1‚â§ai‚â§n1 \leq a_i \leq n1‚â§ai‚Äã‚â§n
1‚â§x‚â§n1 \leq x \leq n1‚â§x‚â§n
Sum of nnn over all testcases is less than 10510^5105
###Sample Input:
2
3 2
2 1 3
3 3
2 1 3
###Sample Output:
YES
1
1 2
NO
###EXPLANATION:
In the first test case, swapping 2 with 1 will sort the array. It is not possible to sort the array in the second test case.
